headline: U.S. crude-by-rail shipments fell by 136,000 bpd in October-EIA
short_text: U.S. crude-by-rail shipments fell by 136,000 bpd in October-EIA — Dec 30 (Reuters) - U.S. shipments of crude oil via rail in October fell by 136,000 barrels per day (bpd) from the previous month to 169,000 bpd, according to data released Thursday by the U.S. Energy Information Administration. Shipments within the United States in October fell by 58,000 barrels per day (bpd) from the previous month to 87,000 bpd, while shipments from Canada to the United states fell by 78,000 barrels per day (bpd) from the previous month to 83,000 bpd. short_text: Russia will not ask EU to end sanctions - RIA — April 1 (Reuters) - Russia will not ask the European Union to end sanctions and has a sufficient "margin of safety", the RIA news agency quoted a Russian foreign ministry official as saying on Friday. "The European Union is not the centre of the universe," Nikolai Kobrinets, the head of the European cooperation department at the ministry, said. short_text: US EPA proposes to extend compliance deadlines for biofuel blending requirements — NEW YORK, Jan 14 (Reuters) - The U.S. Environmental Protection Agency has proposed to extend compliance deadlines for biofuel blending laws, after the agency delayed enforcing compliance because of the coronavirus pandemic. short_text: BUZZ-Street View: Skechers' deal to be smooth sailing despite tariff volatility — ** Popular shoe brand Skechers <SKX.N> has agreed to be taken private by 3G Capital for $9.42 billion, exiting public markets after 26 years, as it grapples with impact of U.S. tariffs ** Deal marks footwear industry's biggest buyout to date ** SKX shares marginally down premarket NO REGULATORY HURDLES EXPECTED ** Deutsche Bank ("hold," PT: $63) sees deal as positive for SKX and expects buyout to provide valuation support for other brands; sees it as potential driver for M&A among other undervalued global names w

headline: Newscasts - US stocks close higher as data keeps Fed rate cut hopes elevated
short_text: Newscasts - US stocks close higher as data keeps Fed rate cut hopes elevated — Click the following link to watch video: https://share.newscasts.refinitiv.com/link?entryId=1_kzjptqa7&referenceId=tag:reuters.com,2025:newsml_RW711603122025RP1_930&pageId=Newscasts Source: 'Reuters - Business videos' Description: U.S. stocks advanced to close higher on Wednesday, as a flurry of economic data kept expectations elevated for an interest rate cut by the Federal Reserve next week, while a fall in Microsoft's shares curbed the advance. headline: Russian apartment block partially collapses in Belgorod, news agencies say
short_text: Russian apartment block partially collapses in Belgorod, news agencies say — MOSCOW, May 12 (Reuters) - A section of a multi-storey apartment block partially collapsed in the Russian city of Belgorod, the RIA Novosti and TASS news agencies reported on Sunday, citing unnamed emergencies services. According to the TASS news agency, two people were injured. The agencies did not say what caused the collapse. (Reporting by Reuters; editing by Guy Faulconbridge) ((moscow.newsroom@thomsonreuters.com))

headline: BUZZ-Boeing falls as FTC requires Spirit AeroSystems asset sales for merger approval
short_text: BUZZ-Boeing falls as FTC requires Spirit AeroSystems asset sales for merger approval — ** Shares of Boeing <BA.N> fall nearly 2.4% to about $200.6 ** The U.S Federal Trade Commission (FTC) requires BA to divest several Spirit AeroSystems <SPR.N> assets to proceed with the merger ** Move comes as part of Boeing’s proposed $8.3 billion acquisition of the airline supplier ** Including session's moves, shares up 13.5% YTD (Reporting by Megavarshini G. Somasundaram in Bengaluru) ((Megavarshini.SomasundaramGnanasundari@thomsonreuters.com))
